2604.25711.title: Learning Generalizable Multimodal Representations for Software Vulnerability Detection
2604.25711.paper_summary: Source code and its accompanying comments are complementary yet naturally aligned modalities-code encodes structural logic while comments capture developer intent. However, existing vulnerability detection methods mostly rely on single-modality code representations, overlooking the complementary semantic information embedded in comments and thus limiting their generalization across complex code structures and logical relationships. To address this, we propose MultiVul, a multimodal contrastive framework that aligns code and comment representations through dual similarity learning and consistency regularization, augmented with diverse code-text pairs to improve robustness. Experiments on widely adopted DiverseVul and Devign datasets across four large language models (LLMs) (i.e., DeepSeek-Coder-6.7B, Qwen2.5-Coder-7B, StarCoder2-7B, and CodeLlama-7B) show that MultiVul achieves up to 27.07% F1 improvement over prompting-based methods and 13.37% over code-only Fine-Tuning, while maintaining comparable inference efficiency.
2604.25711.paper_summary_zh: 源代碼及其附帶的註解是互補的，但自然對齊的模態——代碼編碼結構邏輯，而註解捕捉開發者的意圖。<lb/>然而，現有的漏洞檢測方法大多依賴於單一模態的代碼表示，忽視了嵌入在註解中的互補語義信息，從而限制了它們在複雜代碼結構和邏輯關係中的泛化能力。<lb/>為了解決這個問題，我們提出了MultiVul，一個多模態對比框架，通過雙重相似性學習和一致性正則化來對齊代碼和註解表示，並通過多樣的代碼-文本對來增強穩健性。<lb/>在四個大型語言模型（LLMs）（即DeepSeek-Coder-6.7B、Qwen2.5-Coder-7B、StarCoder2-7B和CodeLlama-7B）上進行的實驗表明，MultiVul在基於提示的方法上實現了高達27.07%的F1改進，並在僅基於代碼的微調上實現了13.37%的改進，同時保持了可比的推理效率。
2604.25711.author: Zeming Dong et.al.
2604.25711.authors: Zeming Dong, Yuejun Guo, Qiang Hu, Yao Zhang, Maxime Cordy, Hao Liu, Mike Papadakis, Yongqiang Lyu
